sniff.en: breathe in through the nose to smell something or make a sniffling sound

ex.en: She sniffed the flowers before buying them.
ex.ko: 그녀는 꽃을 사기 전에 냄새를 맡아 보았다.

snore.en: breathe noisily through the nose and mouth while sleeping

ex.en: He snores loudly every night.
ex.ko: 그는 매일 밤 시끄럽게 코를 곤다.

snore.en: the noise a person makes while snoring

ex.en: His snore kept her awake all night.
ex.ko: 그의 코골이 소리 때문에 그녀는 밤새 잠을 이루지 못했다.

snowball.en: a ball made by pressing snow together

ex.en: The kids threw snowballs at each other.

snowball.en: increase rapidly in size, seriousness, or importance

ex.en: The rumor quickly snowballed into a huge scandal.
ex.ko: 그 소문은 순식간에 큰 스캔들로 눈덩이처럼 불어났다.

so-called.en: used to describe what something or someone is called, often suggesting doubt about whether the name is accurate

ex.en: The so-called expert gave terrible advice.
ex.ko: 그 이른바 전문가라는 사람은 형편없는 조언을 해주었다.

soak.en: leave something in a liquid for a period of time so that it becomes very wet

ex.en: Soak the beans in water overnight.

soaked.en: extremely wet, having absorbed a lot of liquid

ex.en: My shoes are soaked after the rain.
ex.ko: 비가 온 뒤 내 신발이 흠뻑 젖었다.

soaking.en: completely wet, often used before 'wet' for emphasis

ex.en: Come in, you're soaking wet!

soar.en: rise very quickly to a very high level

ex.en: Prices of essential goods soared during the crisis.

soar.en: fly or rise up high into the air smoothly

ex.en: The eagle soared high above the canyon.

sob.en: cry while breathing in short, sudden bursts

ex.en: She sobbed uncontrollably at the funeral.
ex.ko: 그녀는 장례식에서 주체할 수 없이 흐느껴 울었다.

sob.en: a sudden, short breath taken while crying

ex.en: He let out a sob and covered his face.

sociable.en: friendly and enjoying being with other people

ex.en: She's very sociable and makes friends easily.
ex.ko: 그녀는 매우 사교적이라 친구를 쉽게 사귄다.

socialize.en: spend time with others in a friendly way

ex.en: He likes to socialize with his coworkers after work.
ex.ko: 그는 퇴근 후 동료들과 어울리는 것을 좋아한다.

socialize.en: help someone learn to behave in ways that are acceptable in society

ex.en: Puppies need to be socialized with other dogs early on.
ex.ko: 강아지는 어릴 때부터 다른 개들과 사회화되어야 한다.

softness.en: the quality of being soft to the touch

ex.en: I love the softness of this blanket.
ex.ko: 나는 이 담요의 부드러움이 정말 좋다.

softness.en: gentleness and mildness in someone's character or manner

ex.en: There was a softness in her voice that calmed everyone.
ex.ko: 그녀 목소리에는 모두를 진정시키는 온화함이 있었다.

solar.en: relating to the sun or using energy from the sun

ex.en: They installed solar panels on the roof.

solidity.en: the quality of being firm, solid, and not likely to break or change

ex.en: The solidity of the bridge reassured the engineers.

solidity.en: the quality of being reliable, sound, or well-founded

ex.en: Investors questioned the solidity of the company's finances.
ex.ko: 투자자들은 그 회사 재정의 견실함에 의문을 제기했다.

soliloquy.cue: 혼자 속마음을 소리 내어 말하는 (연극 등에서)
soliloquy.en: a speech in which a character speaks their thoughts aloud, alone or as if alone

ex.en: Hamlet's most famous soliloquy begins with 'To be or not to be.'
ex.ko: 햄릿의 가장 유명한 독백은 '사느냐 죽느냐'로 시작한다.

solo.en: done by one person alone, without anyone else

ex.en: She traveled solo across Europe.

solo.en: done or performed by one person alone

ex.en: She launched a solo career after leaving the band.
ex.ko: 그녀는 밴드를 떠난 후 솔로 활동을 시작했다.

solo.en: a piece of music performed by one person alone

ex.en: He played a beautiful guitar solo.

solvent.en: a liquid used to dissolve another substance

ex.en: Water is a common solvent for many substances.
ex.ko: 물은 여러 물질에 대한 흔한 용매다.

solvent.en: having enough money to pay all your debts

ex.en: The company remained solvent despite the recession.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 불황에도 지급 능력을 유지했다.

somewhat.en: to a small or moderate degree; rather

ex.en: The situation is somewhat complicated.
My son-in-law works as an engineer.

sonnet.en: a poem of fourteen lines with a fixed rhyme pattern

ex.en: Shakespeare wrote 154 sonnets.

sophisticated.en: having refined knowledge, tastes, or manners

ex.en: She has a sophisticated sense of style.
ex.ko: 그녀는 세련된 스타일 감각을 가지고 있다.

sophisticated.en: highly developed and complex, especially in technology

ex.en: The company uses sophisticated software to analyze data.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 데이터를 분석하기 위해 정교한 소프트웨어를 사용한다.

soundtrack.en: the recorded music that goes with a film or show

ex.en: I bought the soundtrack of my favorite movie.
ex.ko: 나는 내가 가장 좋아하는 영화의 사운드트랙을 샀다.

ex.en: The birds migrate southward in winter.

soviet.en: relating to the former Soviet Union

ex.en: The Soviet Union dissolved in 1991.

soybean.en: a bean grown for food and oil, high in protein

ex.en: Soybeans are used to make tofu and soy sauce.
ex.ko: 대두는 두부와 간장을 만드는 데 쓰인다.

spa.en: a place where people go to relax, receive beauty treatments, or bathe in hot mineral water

ex.en: We spent the weekend at a spa.

span.en: extend across or over an area or period of time

ex.en: His career spanned four decades.

span.en: the length of time or distance that something covers

ex.en: The bridge has a span of 200 meters.

spare.en: extra and available for use when needed

ex.en: Do you have a spare key to the house?

spare.en: give something, especially time or money, that you could keep for yourself

ex.en: Can you spare a few minutes for me?
ex.ko: 나를 위해 몇 분만 내줄 수 있어?

spare.en: not harm or damage someone or something, especially when you could

ex.en: The soldiers spared the villagers' lives.

sparerib.cue: 살을 대부분 발라낸 돼지 갈비 부위
sparerib.en: a cut of pork ribs with most of the meat trimmed off

ex.en: We grilled sparerib for dinner.

spark.en: a small flash of light or fire, often from electricity or burning

ex.en: A spark from the fire caught the curtain.

spark.en: cause something, especially a strong reaction, to start suddenly

ex.en: The announcement sparked a wave of protests.

sparkling.en: shining brightly with small points of light

ex.en: Her sparkling eyes lit up the room.
ex.ko: 그녀의 반짝이는 눈이 방을 환하게 밝혔다.

sparkling.en: (of a drink) containing bubbles of gas

ex.en: We ordered a bottle of sparkling water.
ex.ko: 우리는 스파클링 워터 한 병을 주문했다.

sparrow.en: a small common bird with brown and grey feathers

ex.en: A sparrow landed on the windowsill.

specialty.en: the particular subject or skill that someone is especially good at or has studied

ex.en: Cardiology is her specialty.

specialty.en: a food or product that a particular place or restaurant is especially known for

ex.en: Seafood pasta is the specialty of this restaurant.
ex.ko: 해산물 파스타가 이 식당의 특별 요리다.

species.en: a group of living things that can breed with each other

ex.en: This species of frog is found only in South America.

specifically.en: in a way that relates to one particular thing, not others

ex.en: I was specifically asked not to mention it.
ex.ko: 나는 그것을 언급하지 말라고 구체적으로 요청받았다.

specify.en: state something clearly and exactly, especially as a requirement

ex.en: Please specify your preferred delivery date.

spectacle.en: an impressive public show or display

ex.en: The fireworks display was a spectacular spectacle.

spectacle.en: an embarrassing situation in which you look foolish in front of others

ex.en: He made a spectacle of himself at the party.
ex.ko: 그는 파티에서 웃음거리가 되는 행동을 했다.

speeder.en: a driver who drives faster than the legal speed limit

ex.en: The police pulled over a speeder on the highway.

spellbound.en: so interested or amazed that you cannot think about anything else

ex.en: The audience sat spellbound throughout the performance.
ex.ko: 관객들은 공연 내내 넋을 잃고 앉아 있었다.

ex.en: The dancer spun gracefully on one foot.
ex.ko: 그 무용수는 한 발로 우아하게 빙빙 돌았다.

spite.en: a desire to hurt, annoy, or offend someone

ex.en: She said it out of pure spite.
ex.ko: 그녀는 순전히 앙심 때문에 그렇게 말했다.

splatter.en: make liquid fly in small drops onto a surface

ex.en: Mud splattered all over my car.

splatter.en: a mark or pattern made by liquid splattering

ex.en: There was a splatter of paint on the floor.

spokesman.en: a man who speaks officially on behalf of a group or organization

ex.en: A company spokesman confirmed the news.

spokeswoman.en: a woman who speaks officially on behalf of a group or organization

ex.en: The spokeswoman denied the allegations.
ex.ko: 그 여성 대변인은 그 혐의를 부인했다.

spray.en: force liquid out in a fine mist onto something

ex.en: She sprayed perfume on her wrist.

spray.en: a liquid kept under pressure in a container so it can be released in fine drops, or the container itself

ex.en: He used a spray to kill the insects.

spreadsheet.en: a computer program used to organize numbers and data in rows and columns

ex.en: She entered the sales figures into a spreadsheet.

sprint.en: a fast run over a short distance

ex.en: She won the 100-meter sprint.

sprint.en: run as fast as you can over a short distance

ex.en: He sprinted to catch the bus.

spur.en: something that urges a person to act or move faster, used both figuratively (encouragement) and literally (a pointed device on a rider's boot)

ex.en: Fear of failure was a spur to hard work.
ex.ko: 실패에 대한 두려움이 열심히 일하게 만드는 자극이 되었다.

spur.en: encourage or stimulate someone or something to do more or better

ex.en: The coach's words spurred the team to victory.
ex.ko: 코치의 말은 팀이 승리하도록 박차를 가했다.

squad.en: a small group of people organized to work together, especially in the military, police, or sports

ex.en: The rescue squad arrived within minutes.

squash.en: press something so hard that it is crushed out of shape

ex.en: Don't sit on the bag, you'll squash the bread.
ex.ko: 가방 위에 앉지 마, 빵이 으깨질 거야.

squash.en: a type of vegetable with thick skin, such as pumpkin or zucchini

ex.en: We roasted some squash for dinner.

squash.cue: 라켓으로 벽에 공을 치는 실내 스포츠
squash.en: an indoor sport in which players hit a small ball against a wall with a racket

ex.en: They play squash together every Friday.
ex.ko: 그들은 매주 금요일마다 함께 스쿼시를 친다.
